description: Test plan for the DebugBuddy Chrome extension

test_strategy.unit_tests: Vitest for pure logic (API client, error parsing, caching, deduplication)
test_strategy.integration_tests: Vitest + chrome-mock for background service worker and storage interactions
test_strategy.component_tests: React Testing Library for popup UI components
test_strategy.e2e_tests: Manual testing on real websites + Puppeteer for automated Chrome extension testing
test_strategy.coverage_target: 80% for src/utils/ and src/api/, functional coverage for UI

cases.title: Debugger attaches to active tab on activation
preconditions: A tab with a webpage is active
steps: Verify chrome.debugger.attach is called with the active tab ID
Verify Runtime.enable and Log.enable commands are sent
cases.expected_result: Debugger is attached and listening for errors

cases.title: Debugger detaches on extension deactivation
preconditions: Debugger is attached to a tab
steps: Close the extension popup or navigate away
cases.expected_result: Debugger is cleanly detached, no orphan sessions

cases.title: Captures uncaught exception (Runtime.exceptionThrown)
steps: Navigate to a page with a JavaScript error (e.g., undefined variable)
cases.expected_result: Error message, stack trace, source URL, and line number are captured

cases.title: Captures console.error() calls
steps: Run console.error('test error') in the page console
cases.expected_result: Console error is captured with message and call site

cases.title: Handles already-attached debugger gracefully
preconditions: Another DevTools instance or extension has the debugger attached
steps: Attempt to attach chrome.debugger to the tab
cases.expected_result: User-friendly error message shown, extension does not crash

cases.title: Handles tab close while debugger is attached
preconditions: Debugger is attached to a tab
cases.expected_result: Extension handles onDetach event, cleans up state without errors

cases.title: Errors are stored in chrome.storage.local
steps: Capture an error via the debugger
cases.expected_result: Error object is persisted with message, stack, source, timestamp, and hash

cases.title: Duplicate errors are grouped by message hash
preconditions: The same error occurs multiple times
cases.expected_result: One entry with count=5, not 5 separate entries

cases.title: Rolling buffer limits storage to 50 errors per tab
preconditions: 50 unique errors are already stored for a tab
cases.expected_result: Oldest error is evicted, total remains at 50

cases.title: Errors persist across popup open/close
cases.expected_result: Previously captured errors are still displayed

cases.title: API client sends correct request format
steps: Call the explain function with an error object
cases.expected_result: Request includes system prompt, error context in user message, and correct headers

cases.title: Explanation is structured (summary, cause, fix, links)
steps: Send a common error (e.g., TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined)
cases.expected_result: Response contains all four sections: summary, cause, fix, links

cases.title: Invalid API key returns user-friendly error
preconditions: API key is invalid or missing
cases.expected_result: User sees 'Invalid API key' message with link to options page

cases.title: Rate limit (429) is handled with retry indication
cases.expected_result: User sees rate limit message, no crash, retry available

cases.title: Network timeout is handled gracefully
cases.expected_result: User sees network error message within 10 seconds

cases.title: Cached responses are returned for repeated errors
preconditions: An error has been explained previously
steps: Request explanation for the same error again
cases.expected_result: Cached response is returned immediately without API call

cases.title: Cache expires after TTL
cases.expected_result: Fresh API call is made, new response is cached

cases.title: Error list renders captured errors
cases.expected_result: Errors displayed in reverse-chronological order with severity icon, message preview, timestamp

cases.title: Empty state is shown when no errors exist
preconditions: No errors in storage for the current tab
cases.expected_result: Helpful message like 'No errors captured yet' is displayed

cases.title: Clicking an error shows detail panel with AI explanation
preconditions: Errors exist, API key is configured
steps: Click on an error in the list
cases.expected_result: Detail panel expands showing full error, stack trace, and AI explanation

cases.title: Loading spinner shows during API call
preconditions: Error is clicked, API call is in progress
steps: Click an error that hasn't been explained yet
cases.expected_result: Spinner/skeleton is visible until response arrives

cases.title: API failure shows error state in detail panel
steps: Click an error when API is unreachable
cases.expected_result: Error message with retry button is shown in the detail panel

cases.title: Copy to clipboard works for error and explanation
preconditions: Detail panel is open with explanation
steps: Click copy button for the error message
Click copy button for the AI explanation
cases.expected_result: Content is copied to clipboard, visual confirmation shown

cases.title: First-run onboarding shows API key prompt
steps: Open the extension popup for the first time
cases.expected_result: Welcome/onboarding screen is shown with API key input, 'Get a key' link, and 'Save & Validate' button

cases.title: Valid API key is accepted and stored
preconditions: Onboarding or options page is open
steps: Paste a valid Claude API key
cases.expected_result: Test request succeeds, key is stored in chrome.storage.local, success indicator shown, onboarding dismisses to error list

cases.title: Invalid API key shows inline error
preconditions: Onboarding or options page is open
cases.expected_result: Validation request returns 401; inline error: 'Invalid API key. Please check and try again.' Key is NOT stored.

cases.title: 'Get a key' link opens Anthropic Console
preconditions: Onboarding or options page is open
cases.expected_result: New browser tab opens to https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys

cases.title: API key is masked by default on options page
cases.expected_result: Key shows as 'sk-ant-•••••••' by default; toggling reveals the full key

cases.title: User can remove their API key
cases.expected_result: Key is deleted from chrome.storage.local, status indicator shows grey/missing, popup reverts to onboarding state

cases.title: Popup shows banner and disables explain when no key
steps: Observe the error list and attempt to click explain
cases.expected_result: Banner shows 'API key required' with link to options; explain button is disabled with tooltip; error list still displays captured errors

cases.title: Network error during key validation shows helpful message
preconditions: Onboarding or options page is open
cases.expected_result: Inline error: 'Could not reach the API. Check your connection.' Key is NOT stored.

cases.title: Key update replaces old key immediately
steps: Enter a new API key on the options page
cases.expected_result: Old key is overwritten; new key is used for subsequent API calls

cases.title: Auto-explain toggle persists across sessions
cases.expected_result: Toggle is still in the 'on' position

cases.title: Capture and explain errors on a site with known JS errors
steps: Navigate to a site with known JavaScript errors
Open popup and view captured errors
Click an error to see explanation
cases.expected_result: Full flow works: capture → display → explain

cases.title: Extension works on CSP-restricted sites
steps: Navigate to a site with strict Content Security Policy (e.g., GitHub)
cases.expected_result: Error capture works (chrome.debugger is not affected by page CSP)

cases.title: Extension handles pages with no errors
steps: Navigate to a clean page with no JS errors
cases.expected_result: Empty state shown, no false positives

cases.title: Extension handles rapid error bursts
steps: Navigate to a page that throws errors in a tight loop (e.g., 100+ errors/sec)
cases.expected_result: Errors are deduplicated, extension remains responsive, no memory leak

cases.title: Badge count updates correctly
steps: Navigate to a page with errors
Observe the badge count on the extension icon
Open the popup to view errors
cases.expected_result: Badge shows error count, clears/updates after viewing
